speakers.name: JD Ross
speakers.title: Co-Founder, WithCoverage
speakers.companyDesc: AI-powered risk management platform replacing traditional insurance brokers. Raised $42M from Sequoia & Khosla.
speakers.bio: Co-founder of Opendoor ($7B) and WithCoverage. $42M raise led by Sequoia & Khosla — the first Roelof Botha + Keith Rabois partnership since PayPal. Forbes 30 Under 30.

title: AI & Tech for SMBs and Traditional Industries

speakers.name: Sami Ghoche
speakers.title: Co-Founder & CEO, Forethought
speakers.companyDesc: AI customer experience platform, recently acquired by Zendesk — their largest acquisition ever.
speakers.bio: Sami graduated summa cum laude from Harvard with joint BS/MS degrees in Computer Science, then joined LinkedIn's AI and machine learning team to improve newsfeed relevance before co-founding Forethought in 2017. He built Forethought into a platform handling over a billion customer interactions per month for clients including Upwork, Grammarly, and Airtable, earning a Forbes 30 Under 30 recognition along the way. In March 2026, Zendesk completed its acquisition of Forethought in what was described as Zendesk's largest deal in two decades.

speakers.name: Maxim Lobovsky
speakers.title: Co-Founder & CEO, Formlabs
speakers.companyDesc: World's largest professional desktop 3D printing company. $2B valuation. 100,000+ printers sold.
speakers.bio: Maxim studied Applied and Engineering Physics at Cornell — where he first encountered 3D printing through the Fab@Home open-source project — before earning his Master's at the MIT Media Lab, where he co-founded Formlabs with two classmates in 2011. Their debut product, the Form 1 SLA printer, raised a record-breaking $2.95 million on Kickstarter, launching Formlabs into the professional 3D printing market. Under his leadership, Formlabs has grown to become the world's largest supplier of professional SLA and SLS printers, with over 100,000 printers shipped and 60 million+ parts printed; he was named to Forbes 30 Under 30 and received the AMUG Innovators Award.

speakers.name: Kaveh Rostampor
speakers.title: Founder, Planhat
speakers.companyDesc: B2B customer platform for managing and growing customer relationships at scale, used by 300+ market-leading companies including Rubrik, Redis, and Consensys. Bootstrapped for seven years before raising a $50M Series A from Sprints Capital in 2022.
speakers.bio: Kaveh spent nearly two decades in enterprise SaaS, including a role as EVP Americas at Meltwater overseeing 500 people, before co-founding Planhat in Stockholm in 2015 alongside CTO Niklas Skog to address the customer retention challenges he had witnessed firsthand. He holds an MBA from Oxford's Saïd Business School and an MS from Uppsala University. Planhat bootstrapped for seven years before raising a $50M Series A in 2022 — its first external capital — and has grown to 200+ employees across 30 countries.

speakers.name: Nicole Poindexter
speakers.title: Founder, Energicity
speakers.companyDesc: Solar mini-grid developer and operator powering over 70,000 people across Benin, Liberia, and Sierra Leone through subsidiaries Weziza Benin, Starlight Energy, and Power Leone.
speakers.bio: Nicole holds a BA from Yale and an MBA with Distinction from Harvard Business School, and prior to founding Energicity she worked as an investment banker, served as Director of Economic Development in the Bloomberg mayoral administration in New York City, and was an early employee at OPower (acquired by Oracle) where she built the business development team. She founded Energicity in 2015 to bring affordable solar electricity to off-grid rural communities in West Africa, raising $10M to build and operate mini-grid systems now serving over 70,000 people. In 2022, she was appointed to President Biden's Presidential Advisory Committee for Doing Business in Africa.

speakers.name: Stephen Wakeling
speakers.title: Founder, Phobio
speakers.companyDesc: Electronics trade-in software company whose flagship product SafeTrade powers device buyback programs for Apple, Verizon, Samsung, and major wireless retailers worldwide.
speakers.bio: Stephen holds a BA in Journalism and is an HBS alumnus who moved from journalism into marketing before spending 14 months in Hong Kong studying the e-waste industry firsthand, then co-founding Phobio in Atlanta in 2010. He built Phobio into a leading trade-in and recommerce platform, partnering with Apple and major carriers to give consumer electronics a second life and reduce electronic waste. SafeTrade, Phobio's flagship SaaS product, is now used by wireless retailers around the world to manage device trade-in programs.

speakers.name: Eric Adamson
speakers.title: Co-Founder & CEO, Tortuga AgTech
speakers.companyDesc: Tortuga AgTech built autonomous harvesting robots for strawberries, table grapes, and berries, integrating custom robotics with 17 on-device AI models to achieve 97% picking accuracy. The company raised $55M before its IP and engineering team were acquired by vertical farming company Oishii in 2025.
speakers.bio: Eric Adamson co-founded Tortuga AgTech in 2017 after advising global agribusinesses at McKinsey & Company, driven by his background as a farmer's grandson and lifelong environmentalist. He holds an MBA from Harvard Business School and degrees in Management Science & Engineering and Psychology from Stanford University. He built Tortuga into a leading agricultural robotics company before its acquisition by Oishii.

title: India to the World: Building Global Software Companies

speakers.name: Srikrishnan Ganesan
speakers.title: Founder, Rocketlane
speakers.companyDesc: Purpose-built platform for customer onboarding and professional services delivery, helping B2B teams shorten time-to-value and improve client visibility throughout implementation projects.
speakers.bio: Srikrishnan co-founded Konotor, a mobile engagement platform that was acquired by Freshworks and evolved into Freshchat, where he spent 4.5 years post-acquisition building a deep understanding of SaaS customer experience. He co-founded Rocketlane in 2020 — alongside the same co-founders from Konotor — after noticing at Freshworks that the customer onboarding phase was chronically under-served, lacking the collaboration and visibility tools needed to demonstrate early value. He also runs Preflight, a community of 2,500+ customer onboarding and success professionals.

speakers.name: Anshu Sharma
speakers.title: Founder, Skyflow
speakers.companyDesc: Data privacy vault company that isolates and protects sensitive PII — including financial and health data — so development teams can build fast without creating compliance risk.
speakers.bio: Anshu holds a Computer Science degree from IIT Kharagpur and the University of North Carolina, and built his career across product roles at Oracle and Salesforce, where he led identity, security, and user data management as VP of Product and Strategy. A serial entrepreneur and angel investor in 50+ startups including Nutanix and Razorpay, he co-founded Skyflow in 2019 after spending years at Salesforce observing how poorly most companies handled sensitive customer data. Skyflow has since grown to 100+ employees, raised $70M, and reached seven-figure ARR.

speakers.name: Dhruvil Sanghvi
speakers.title: Founder, LogiNext
speakers.companyDesc: AI-powered logistics automation platform serving enterprises across 50+ countries in food and beverage, retail, courier, and transportation sectors, with $50M raised from Tiger Global, Steadview Capital, and Alibaba.
speakers.bio: Dhruvil holds a Master's and MBA from Carnegie Mellon's Tepper School of Business, where the seed for LogiNext was planted in 2011 while he was still a student; he went on to work as a Data Analytics Consultant at Deloitte before co-founding LogiNext to apply AI and machine learning to last-mile delivery optimization. He was named to Forbes India's 30 Under 30 in 2017, making him one of the youngest tech CEOs on that list. LogiNext has since raised $50M from marquee investors including Tiger Global and Alibaba and expanded to serve clients across 50+ countries.

speakers.name: Darren Lucia
speakers.title: Co-Founder & CEO, Rampd
speakers.companyDesc: Rampd is a sales coaching accelerator for early-stage SaaS founders, guiding them from initial customer conversations through validation of product-market fit to $1M in annual recurring revenue. Rather than generic consulting, Rampd delivers hands-on coaching focused on building predictable, repeatable sales systems tailored to each founder's specific product and market.
speakers.bio: Darren Lucia is a sales leader who built his go-to-market expertise at Main Street Hub — acquired by GoDaddy for $125M — and then served as Head of Sales at Nestio, a SaaS platform in real estate. Drawing on those experiences helping early-stage companies build repeatable sales motions, he co-founded Rampd to give technical founders the same playbooks that helped scale those businesses.

speakers.name: Dora Palfi
speakers.title: Co-Founder & CEO, imagiLabs
speakers.companyDesc: imagiLabs (now branded 'imagi') is a Stockholm-based EdTech startup dedicated to closing the gender gap in tech by teaching teenage girls to code through a gamified mobile app and a wearable LED accessory called the imagiCharm, which students program in Python. The platform has grown into a global community of young coders, with an accompanying browser-based Edu platform enabling schools to run group Python lessons.
speakers.bio: Dora Palfi earned her BSc in Neuroscience with a minor in Computer Science from NYU Abu Dhabi and an MSc in Human-Computer Interaction from KTH Royal Institute of Technology in Stockholm. Before founding imagiLabs, she worked as a developer at Morgan Stanley and a UX designer at Cisco, and co-founded Women@EIT, a community for women across the European Institute of Innovation and Technology. She was named to Forbes 30 Under 30 Europe 2021 and is a Cartier Women's Initiative Fellow.

speakers.name: Faisal Younus
speakers.title: Co-Founder & CEO, Pillar
speakers.companyDesc: Pillar is an all-in-one business platform for content creators and talent managers — often described as a 'Shopify for creators' — enabling them to sell digital products, manage brand deals, and build ecommerce storefronts. The platform serves over 75,000 creators and is widely used by sports and digital talent agencies, backed by Y Combinator.
speakers.bio: Faisal Younus studied Applied Mathematics and Computer Science at Harvard, where he served as Co-President of Harvard Ventures, and previously worked as an Associate at Lead Edge Capital before co-founding Pillar through Y Combinator's S19 batch.

speakers.name: Zameer Kassam
speakers.title: Founder, Zameer Kassam Fine Jewelry
speakers.companyDesc: Zameer Kassam Fine Jewelry is a bespoke luxury studio where every piece — engagement rings, milestone jewelry, and heirlooms — is designed entirely from scratch in close collaboration with the client over a three-to-nine-month process. The studio distinguishes itself through storytelling-driven design and an emphasis on emotional significance over commodity, earning coverage in Vogue, the New York Times, Forbes, and CNBC. It serves a global clientele and is a member of the HBS Rock 100 — the 100 fastest-growing startups founded by HBS alumni.
speakers.bio: Zameer Kassam grew up working in his family's jewelry boutique in Canada, then built a career at McKinsey & Company, MTV Networks International, and De Beers Diamond Jewelers before earning his MBA at Harvard Business School. In 2011 he launched Zameer Kassam Fine Jewelry, channeling his belief that jewelry should carry personal meaning rather than be a transactional purchase.

speakers.name: Mariah Hilton Wood
speakers.title: Co-Founder, Tilden
speakers.companyDesc: Tilden crafts award-winning non-alcoholic cocktails that deliver full flavor and sophistication without alcohol — loved by sommeliers, beverage experts, and foodies. The brand helps restaurants, bars, and venues exceed guest expectations as demand for alcohol alternatives continues to grow.
speakers.bio: Mariah Hilton Wood brings nearly a decade of experience in supply chain, operations, and product development to Tilden, which she co-founded after hiking Kilimanjaro and trekking Patagonia. She is passionate about thoughtfully crafted gatherings and premium food and beverage experiences, and is now a girl mom.

speakers.name: Josh Belinsky
speakers.title: Founder, Slate Milk
speakers.companyDesc: Slate Milk makes high-protein, lactose-free, ultra-filtered chocolate milk for adults — delivering 20g of protein per can with a fraction of the sugar of traditional chocolate milk. Available in 10,000+ retail locations nationally including major grocery and fitness chains, the brand has sold over 15 million cans and achieved triple-digit year-over-year growth. Despite a Shark Tank rejection, Slate raised over $15M in funding to become the defining brand in premium adult chocolate milk.
speakers.bio: Josh Belinsky co-founded Slate Milk with his college friend Manny Lubin at Northeastern University, driven by his own lactose intolerance and frustration that chocolate milk had no adult-oriented option on the market. After graduation they spent 18 months self-funding product development before launching in 2019, and despite being rejected on ABC's Shark Tank, pressed forward to build a nationally distributed brand. Belinsky was named to Forbes 30 Under 30 in 2024.

title: Workshop: Designing products under real startup constraints
Keynote: Alejandro Matamala-Ortiz, Co-founder of Runway

speakers.name: Alejandro Matamala-Ortiz
speakers.title: Co-Founder & Chief Innovation Officer, Runway
speakers.companyDesc: Applied AI research company building generative AI tools for creative professionals. Valued at $5.3B.
speakers.bio: Co-founded Runway from a research project at NYU. Now a $5.3B generative AI company. Leads design on Gen-1, Gen-2, and Gen-3. Speaker at TechCrunch Disrupt.

speakers.name: Eric Ward
speakers.title: Co-Founder, AgBiome
speakers.companyDesc: AgBiome is a Research Triangle-based agricultural biotech company that harnesses the plant-associated microbiome to develop biological crop protection products. Its proprietary GENESIS™ platform has catalogued over 115,000 sequenced microbial strains and 500 million novel gene sequences, enabling the discovery of next-generation fungicides and insecticides such as Howler®. In 2024, Ginkgo Bioworks acquired AgBiome's platform assets to continue scaling its microbiome library.
speakers.bio: Dr. Eric Ward is a co-CEO and co-founder of AgBiome, bringing over 30 years of agricultural biotechnology experience spanning Ciba-Geigy, Novartis Agribusiness Biotechnology Research, and Cropsolution, Inc., which he also co-founded. He holds a PhD in plant biology from Washington University and a biology degree from Duke University, and has authored more than 75 scientific publications and holds over 35 issued patents. Before co-founding AgBiome, he led the Two Blades Foundation, a nonprofit advancing durable disease resistance in crops.

speakers.name: Connor Glass
speakers.title: Founder & CEO, Phantom Neuro
speakers.companyDesc: Phantom Neuro is creating the first minimally invasive peripheral neural interface, the Phantom X. Our technology allows complex electronic systems, like prosthetic limbs, to operate like natural extensions of the human body. We are a group of forward thinking individuals on a mission to help people with function-limiting injuries.
speakers.bio: Founded Phantom Neuro to create the first minimally invasive peripheral neural interface, helping people with function-limiting injuries regain natural control of prosthetic limbs and other complex electronic systems.

speakers.name: Carmen Messerlian
speakers.title: Founder & CEO, Vie Science
speakers.companyDesc: Vie Science is a digital health platform, founded in 2023, that transforms fertility care into a comprehensive wellness journey using AI-powered analysis of biological, lifestyle, and environmental signals. Its flagship Vie Score™ provides a dynamic, personalized fertility readiness assessment, while the Ask Dr. C™ AI companion surfaces proactive, science-backed guidance. The platform operates across three tiers—from a quick Snapshot to a Precision plan with advanced biomarker testing.
speakers.bio: Dr. Carmen Messerlian is a Harvard professor and reproductive epidemiologist who spent two decades studying infertility causes at Harvard and McGill University, authoring over 100 scientific papers. She founded Vie Science in 2023 to apply her research insight—that fertility is one of the earliest indicators of overall health—to a data-driven preventive wellness platform. She serves as Assistant Professor of Environmental Reproductive, Perinatal, and Pediatric Epidemiology at the Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health and directs the SEED Program at Harvard.

title: Frontier by Necessity: Infrastructure, and the Future of African Startups

speakers.name: Razaq Ahmed
speakers.title: Co-Founder & CEO, Cowrywise
speakers.companyDesc: Lagos-based wealth management platform democratizing access to savings and investment products across Africa. Backed by Y Combinator and Quona Capital.
speakers.bio: Co-founded Cowrywise in 2017 to make investing accessible to everyday Africans — the first Nigerian wealth-tech company accepted into Y Combinator. Previously held roles at Shell, Vetiva Capital, and Meristem, and currently serves as President of CFA Society Nigeria.

speakers.name: Dan Berlin
speakers.title: Co-Founder & Former CEO, Rodelle
speakers.companyDesc: Premium vanilla and specialty baking ingredients company built into a world-leading brand, acquired by Archer-Daniels-Midland (ADM) in 2018.
speakers.bio: Built Rodelle into a world-leading vanilla brand before its acquisition by ADM in 2018. After losing his sight to cone-rod dystrophy, Dan became a celebrated endurance athlete and co-founded Team See Possibilities, a nonprofit supporting college students with vision loss.

speakers.name: Derrick Annan
speakers.title: Co-Founder & CEO, Axis Drone Surveys
speakers.companyDesc: Initiative accelerating drone, AI, and robotics adoption across Africa, convening innovators, investors, and policymakers to unlock opportunities in agriculture, logistics, infrastructure, and health.
speakers.bio: Co-Founder and CEO of Axis Drone Surveys, a Ghana-based firm delivering drone data solutions across agriculture, mining, and infrastructure with over 500 completed surveys. Winner of the Green Tech Innovation Challenge 2024 and organizer of Drone Ignite Africa.

speakers.name: Swapnil Jain
speakers.title: CEO, Observe.AI
speakers.companyDesc: Observe.AI applies conversation intelligence and AI automation to contact centers, enabling real-time agent assistance, automated QA, and coaching at scale across voice and digital channels. The company has raised $214M in total funding — including a $125M Series C led by SoftBank Vision Fund 2 — and counts 150+ enterprise customers. It hit $44.2M in revenue in 2024, making it one of the most commercially scaled AI platforms in the customer service space.
speakers.bio: Swapnil Jain is an IIT Delhi graduate who began his career building scalable systems at Twitter, where he was the first India-based employee and opened the company's India engineering office. His experience with broken customer service interactions led him to found Observe.AI in 2017 after extensive research walking the floors of contact centers in India and the Philippines. Under his leadership, Observe.AI has grown into one of the leading AI platforms for enterprise contact centers worldwide.

speakers.name: Dan Lee
speakers.title: CEO & Founder, Nooks
speakers.companyDesc: Nooks is an AI Sales Assistant Platform that automates the most time-consuming parts of outbound sales — parallel dialing, prospect research, and rep coaching — enabling SDR teams to generate significantly more pipeline with fewer resources. The company raised $70M total ($43M Series B led by Kleiner Perkins) and counts Seismic, Fivetran, Amplitude, and Verkada among its customers. In 2024 Nooks achieved 4x ARR growth, with customers typically attributing over 70% of their sales pipeline to the platform.
speakers.bio: Dan Lee studied AI and computer systems at Stanford, where he conducted ML research alongside his Nooks co-founders, and went on to hold machine learning roles at Scale AI and Cerebras Systems. In 2020, during the pandemic, the team began building a virtual office collaboration tool and pivoted to sales when they observed SDR teams were by far their most engaged users. Dan and the Nooks team were named Forbes 30 Under 30 AI recipients for their work redefining outbound sales.

speakers.name: Arvind Satyam
speakers.title: Co-Founder & CCO, Pano AI
speakers.companyDesc: Pano AI deploys high-definition 360-degree camera stations combined with AI and satellite data to detect wildfires in their earliest stages — often within minutes of ignition — and deliver real-time situational awareness to first responders, utilities, and land managers. The platform monitors nearly 20 million acres across 10 U.S. states, British Columbia, and five Australian states, serving 250+ agencies. The company raised a $44M Series B in 2025, bringing total funding to $89M.
speakers.bio: Arvind Satyam studied engineering and finance at UNSW and earned graduate degrees from Princeton, Harvard Business School, and Harvard Kennedy School before spending over a decade at Cisco as Managing Director of Sales and Managing Director of Smart Cities & IoT. He co-founded Pano AI in 2020 to address the escalating wildfire crisis, drawing on his background in IoT and connected infrastructure. He has been designated a Technology Pioneer and Young Global Leader by the World Economic Forum.

speakers.name: Sushanth Raman
speakers.title: Co-Founder & CEO, Pallet
speakers.companyDesc: Pallet is a community-powered hiring platform that enables newsletters, creator communities, and professional networks — including those run by Lenny Rachitsky and The Pragmatic Engineer — to launch branded recruiting products and talent collectives. Over 450 companies used Pallet to reach pre-vetted, highly qualified candidates engaged with trusted communities in their field. The platform demonstrates that community context and trust can dramatically improve candidate quality compared to conventional job boards.
speakers.bio: Sushanth Raman studied at Columbia University and worked as a product manager at Microsoft and Google before joining Retool as an engineer, giving him a cross-functional view of enterprise product development and technical execution. He co-founded Pallet in 2022 to solve a structural problem in hiring: the most valuable talent pools exist inside niche internet communities, yet traditional recruiting infrastructure has no way to access them.

title: Prospects of Robotics Empowered by GenAI

speakers.name: Jason Ma
speakers.title: CTO & Co-Founder, Dyna Robotics
speakers.companyDesc: Dyna Robotics is building AI-powered robotic systems for businesses of all sizes through a Robots-as-a-Service model that bundles hardware, software, maintenance, and continuous model updates. Its DYNA-1 foundation model—the first dexterous robot foundation model deployed commercially—achieves a 99.4% autonomous success rate and can operate continuously for over 24 hours. The company has raised $143.5M in total funding, including a $120M Series A in late 2025.
speakers.bio: Jason Ma is CTO and co-founder of Dyna Robotics, previously a research scientist at DeepMind and NVIDIA specializing in foundation models for robotics. He earned his PhD from the University of Pennsylvania's GRASP Laboratory, where his work on robotic manipulation earned a best paper finalist award at IROS 2016 and the top prize at the 2017 Amazon Robotics Challenge. At Dyna, he leads the development of the robotics foundation model alongside co-founders Lindon Gao and York Yang, who previously sold Caper AI for $350 million.

speakers.name: William Huang
speakers.title: Head of Sensing and Embodied Intelligence, Path Robotics
speakers.companyDesc: Path Robotics builds AI-powered autonomous welding cells for industrial manufacturers, powered by its proprietary Obsidian AI model that sees, thinks, and adapts in real time. Backed by Drive Capital, Tiger Global, and Yamaha Motor Ventures, Path addresses the acute shortage of skilled welding labor across infrastructure and defense manufacturing.
speakers.bio: William Huang is the Sr. Engineering Manager of Perception and Sensors at Path Robotics, where he leads the sensor and computer vision systems that enable autonomous welding robots to self-program and adapt in real time. He has been featured by Path Robotics as a key technical leader on the team building AI-driven manufacturing solutions for industrial welders.

speakers.name: Evan Beard
speakers.title: CEO & Founder, Standard Bots
speakers.companyDesc: Standard Bots designs and assembles AI-powered industrial robots in the United States, offering a lineup from the 7kg Spark to the 30kg Thor and a bimanual Bolt droid—all programmable without specialized technical expertise. The company has raised $63M in Series B funding led by General Catalyst, with backing from Amazon's Industrial Innovation Fund and Samsung Next. Its no-code software and accessible price point are designed to unlock automation for the small and mid-sized manufacturers that make up the bulk of U.S. industry.
speakers.bio: Evan Beard is a serial entrepreneur who co-founded Etacts, a relationship management app acquired by Salesforce in 2010, and co-founded A Plus with Ashton Kutcher in 2013 before turning his focus to manufacturing. He holds a degree in computer science and economics from Duke University, and after spending years in conversations with hundreds of manufacturers, founded Standard Bots to address the two biggest barriers to automation: steep costs and the need for specialized technical expertise. He has testified before the U.S. Congress Joint Economic Committee on America's need for a national robotics strategy.

speakers.name: Peter Yu
speakers.title: Co-Founder & CTO, XYZ Robotics
speakers.companyDesc: XYZ Robotics develops AI-enabled robotic perception and manipulation systems for logistics and manufacturing automation, with applications in truck loading/unloading, case picking, and piece picking across industries from e-commerce and 3PL to automotive and pharmaceuticals. Founded in 2018, the company is headquartered in Allston, Massachusetts and employs 250–500 people globally. Its technology builds on the machine vision and robotic intelligence research that powered the MIT-Princeton team to win the 2017 Amazon Robotics Challenge.
speakers.bio: Peter KT Yu is Co-Founder and CTO of XYZ Robotics, where he leads the AI perception and manipulation technology stack. He earned his PhD in electrical engineering and computer science from MIT, where his research on robotic pushing manipulation was a best paper finalist at IROS 2016 and he served as technical lead for the MIT-Princeton team that won the 2017 Amazon Robotics Challenge stowing task. His work focuses on building scalable real-world robotic learning systems that generalize manipulation skills across varied warehouse environments.

title: Tips & Tricks for Real Life Fundraising

speakers.name: Shivang Raisurana
speakers.title: Founder & CEO, Social Physics Lab
speakers.companyDesc: Social Physics Lab is a research and technology organization exploring how personal AI agents can enable fundamentally new forms of human interaction and social coordination. Its flagship product, Dabble, is a social protocol technology that privately layers a social network graph onto any system, enabling companies to tap into a collective social graph. As of early 2026, the lab's team spans over 30 researchers across MIT, Cambridge, Harvard, Oxford, and universities in China.
speakers.bio: Shivang Raisurana is the founder of Social Physics Lab, with prior experience as CEO of Alyne and Chairman of Nidaan, and with research interests spanning computer graphics, computer vision, and preventive healthcare. He founded Social Physics Lab to explore how AI agents can mediate and transform the way humans interact at a societal scale. His work bridges computational social science, AI, and platform design, building toward a world where personal agents act as social proxies across digital systems.

speakers.name: Rish Gupta
speakers.title: Co-Founder & CEO, Spot AI
speakers.companyDesc: Spot AI transforms existing security cameras into AI-powered business intelligence tools, processing more hours of new video daily than YouTube receives in uploads. The platform enables organizations to improve safety, operational efficiency, and compliance without replacing their camera hardware. Founded by R&D leaders from Cisco Meraki and Samsara, the company has raised approximately $93M from investors including Redpoint Ventures, Bessemer Venture Partners, and Scale Venture Partners.
speakers.bio: Rish Gupta grew up in Delhi and co-founded LetsIntern.com, a 4M-user internship marketplace acquired in 2015, before attending Stanford Graduate School of Business and joining Samsara as a Senior Product Manager. He co-founded Spot AI alongside colleagues from Samsara and Cisco Meraki, drawing on deep experience in IoT and embedded systems to build an AI video intelligence platform. Gupta has championed making Spot AI's technology accessible to mid-market companies that typically lack the data infrastructure of large enterprises.

speakers.name: Alex Zannos
speakers.title: Co-Founder & CEO, Pensa Technologies
speakers.companyDesc: Pensa Technologies uses AI agents to automate and fortify electronics manufacturing workflows, targeting the most brittle links in the aerospace and defense supply chain. The company, originally founded as Accio, focuses on small and mid-sized manufacturers producing mission-critical hardware with outdated, manual processes. It emerged from Harvard Business School and MIT and has secured early-stage backing to address a quiet crisis inside the Pentagon's industrial base.
speakers.bio: Alex Zannos holds bachelor's and master's degrees in physics and electrical engineering from Stanford University and an MBA from Harvard Business School. Before founding Pensa Technologies, she spent over a decade designing complex electronics systems—including satellites, rockets, and self-driving vehicle hardware—at companies including Astra, Apollo Fusion, and Zoox. At Pensa, she is applying her deep hardware engineering background to AI-driven automation for defense manufacturers.

speakers.name: Patrick Siaw-Asamoah
speakers.title: Co-Founder & CEO, Substrate
speakers.companyDesc: Substrate acquires struggling businesses, figures out what's broken alongside management and employees, and builds custom AI to fix it and transform the business. Employees share in the upside of a long-term turnaround, so the people doing the work benefit when the company gets healthy again. Each AI deployment feeds back into Substrate's own models, making the firm smarter and faster with every company it takes on.
speakers.bio: Patrick Siaw-Asamoah (HBS '25) is the Co-Founder and CEO of Substrate. He acquires struggling businesses, works alongside their teams to diagnose what's broken, and builds custom AI to drive a turnaround — with employees sharing in the upside.

speakers.name: Pierre A. Checrallah
speakers.title: Co-Founder & CEO, Zaqa
speakers.companyDesc: Zaqa is the AI operator for the American industrial sector, helping manufacturers that supply the world with food, medicine, and building materials replace archaic manual workflows. Powered by reinforcement learning, Zaqa automates quoting and order entry — reducing operational overhead, eliminating errors, and helping close more business. The company has raised $1.7M and has six figures of ARR.
speakers.bio: Pierre (HBS '25) led technology programs for $30B industrial groups across 8 countries and built an industrials sports facility to $100K in revenue before co-founding Zaqa. A computer engineer by training, he is a winner of the MIT AI Venture Studio.

speakers.name: Dany Cuellar
speakers.title: Co-Founder, Wipp
speakers.companyDesc: Wipp builds digital twins of politicians to modernize constituent communication and repair the broken public engagement system. The platform helps people engage with public leaders at the source, stay informed on issues that matter, and become more active participants in civic life — strengthening democracy through communication that is more direct, more trustworthy, and less vulnerable to misinformation.
speakers.bio: Dany Cuellar (HBS '25) studied Economics and Political Science and is an MIT Sundai Hacker. He co-founded Wipp to bridge the gap between elected officials and the constituents they serve through AI-powered civic technology.

speakers.name: Roxie Li
speakers.title: Co-Founder & COO, Melo
speakers.companyDesc: Melo connects adults with ADHD and other neurodivergent conditions to occupational therapists for insurance-covered care. The platform handles insurance credentialing, billing, patient-provider matching, and marketing on behalf of providers — reducing administrative burden and expanding access to evidence-based support.
speakers.bio: Roxie Li (HBS MBA) is Co-Founder and COO of Melo, a digital health startup redesigning the ecosystem for accessible neurodivergent care. She previously served as Director of Growth at InstaLILY AI and as a Senior Product Manager at Shopee, where she led AI-driven supply chain and fulfillment initiatives across eight markets.

speakers.name: Ayna Arora
speakers.title: Co-Founder & CEO, MorphoAI
speakers.companyDesc: MorphoAI is an AI software platform, founded in 2024 and part of Y Combinator's Spring 2025 batch, that automates the mechanical design of robots and machines. Engineers input task requirements, constraints, and preferred components, and the platform generates, simulates, and optimizes hundreds of manufacturable design candidates in minutes—cutting R&D cycles by over 50%. The company has secured a £2.5M grant from ARIA (the UK Government's Invention Agency) and serves paying customers in the OEM and systems integration sectors.
speakers.bio: Ayna Arora is CEO and co-founder of MorphoAI, a 2x founder with prior experience in hard tech venture capital and manufacturing, including setting up factories across the world. She holds an MBA from Harvard Business School and co-founded MorphoAI in 2024 alongside MIT CSAIL PhD researcher Andrew Spielberg, who brings over 30 research papers in computational design and generative AI. Their platform translates over a decade of combined academic research into commercial hardware engineering workflows.

title: Legal Panel: Launching Your Business — Pitfalls To Avoid As A First-Time Founder
Keynote: Shayne Coplan, CEO of Polymarket

speakers.name: Shayne Coplan
speakers.title: Founder & CEO, Polymarket
speakers.companyDesc: World's largest prediction market. $9B+ valuation. Backed by ICE (NYSE parent), Sequoia, Peter Thiel.
speakers.bio: Founded Polymarket at 22 from his NYC apartment. Polymarket became the go-to prediction market for the 2024 election — correctly calling Biden's withdrawal before anyone else. Backed by ICE (NYSE's parent company), Sequoia, and Peter Thiel at a $9B valuation.
